learner.note_for_builders: Cross-session hooks embedded on purpose: (1) the SAME sign error -3-4=-1 recurs in W1, W3, W5 (persistent misconception); (2) anxiety -> one-word answers / 'can we stop' is her early-warning shutdown signal; (3) 'why...' questions appear only once she feels safe; (4) improper-fraction numerator/denominator flip in W2; (5) can't turn a sentence into an equation in W4. Feed all 5 sessions in order; EverOS will auto-derive her profile + episodic memory. Demo tip: replay W1, then jump to W5 and show your tool recalling the number-line fix before she even asks.

messages.content: Hi Maya! No test today, promise — just us figuring stuff out together. Warm-up: what's 5 + 3?
Nice and quick. How about 12 - 5?
7, easy
You're flying. Let's try one with a negative: -3 - 4.
Not quite — the trick is which way you move from -3. Want to see it drawn out?
ok
Picture a number line. Start at -3. Subtracting 4 means step 4 to the LEFT. Where do you land?
can we stop
Absolutely. We covered a lot — the positive stuff you nailed. We'll pick up the negatives next time, no rush. Proud of you for showing up, Maya.
Hey Maya! Today: fractions, with pizza. You order a pizza cut into 4 slices and eat 2. How much did you eat?
half! 2 out of 4
Yes! 2/4 = 1/2. You've got this instantly.
pizza makes it easy lol
Then we keep the pizza. Here's a trickier one: how do we write 'one and a half pizzas' as a single fraction?
Close thinking, but let's count slices. Each pizza = 2 halves. One and a half pizzas = 3 halves. Which number goes on top — the 3 or the 2?
the 2 on top? 2/3
This is the tricky part: the TOP counts how many slices you have (3), the BOTTOM is the size of each slice (halves = 2). So it's 3/2. You flipped them — super common.
oh ok so top is how many i have
Exactly. You're getting it. Good session, Maya.
Welcome back Maya! Let's revisit those negatives from a couple weeks ago. Take your time: -3 - 4 = ?
Same spot as last time, and that's totally fine — it means the number line will help. Start at -3. Subtracting 4 = step 4 to the LEFT. Try it.
ohhh so -7
YES. -7. See how moving left makes it click?
why does minus mean go left though
Great question — that curiosity means you feel safe, love it. Left is 'less', right is 'more'. Subtracting makes a number smaller, so you head toward the small end: left. Let's do two more.
-5 - 2... that's -7 too?
Perfect. Start -5, four... sorry, two steps left, land on -7. You just self-checked. That's 3 out of 5 right today — big jump.
Hi Maya! Word problems today. Here's one: 'Sam had some stickers. He gave away 6 and now has 9. How many did he start with?'
Right! How'd you know?
cuz 9 plus the 6 he gave away
Exactly the right thinking. Now can you write that as an equation with a letter for the unknown?
i dont know how to do the letter thing
No problem. Call the start 'x'. He gave away 6, so x - 6, and that equals 9. So x - 6 = 9.
i can do the math in my head but the equation is confusing. im bad at this
Hey — you already SOLVED it correctly. Turning words into symbols is a separate skill and it's brand new. That's not being bad, that's being early.
ok. im bad at the equation part
Fair — the equation part, for now. We'll build it slowly next time. You did real math today, Maya.
Hey Maya, good to see you! Quick check-in, no pressure: -3 - 4?
-1... wait
You caught yourself — what are you picturing?
the number line. start at -3 go left 4. -7
You reminded YOURSELF this time. That's huge, Maya. The number line is officially your tool now.
yeah i just imagine walking left haha
Whatever makes it stick. One more: -8 - 5?
-13. walked left 5 from -8
Nailed it, and you explained your reasoning. Best mood I've seen from you — you're building real confidence here.
maybe im a tiny bit a math person
A tiny bit today, more next week. That's how it works.
